name_en: Kagura Dance of Inabayama Shrine, Nagaoka
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property, the kagura (sacred dance) of Inabayama Shrine in Nagaoka. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-11-02. Maintained by the Nagaoka Kagura Preservation Society (Nagaoka).

name_romaji: Shimōsa no Kuni Katori-gun Fuma-gō Mizuchō
name_en: Land Survey Register of Fuma Village, Katori District, Shimōsa Province
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the old documents category, a land survey register (mizuchō) of Fuma village, Katori district, Shimōsa Province. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-11-02. Privately held (Shidaka).

name_en: Works of Takeuchi Tōhaku (Writings)
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the old documents category, writings documenting the achievements of Takeuchi Tōhaku. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-11-02. Privately held (Nissato).

name_en: Main Sanctuary of Kubo Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the main sanctuary (honden) of Kubo Shrine. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-12-06. Located at Kubo Shrine (Kubo).

name_en: Portrait of Chiba Chikatake
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, a portrait of Chiba Chikatake. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-12-06. Held by Kubo Shrine (Kubo).

name_en: Illustrated Scroll of the Sacred Procession of Kubo Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, an illustration of the sacred procession (shinkō) of Kubo Shrine. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-12-06. Held by Kubo Shrine (Kubo).

name_en: Calligraphy of the Sacred Name by the Priest Yūten
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the calligraphy category, calligraphy of the sacred name (myōgō) by the priest Yūten. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-12-06. Held by the Kubo district (Kubo).

name_en: Memorial Tablets Related to the Uchida Clan, Lords of the Omigawa Domain
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, memorial tablets (ihai) related to the Uchida clan, lords of the Omigawa domain. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2005-12-06. Held by Honganji Temple (Omigawa).

name_en: Materials Related to Inō Tadataka
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, materials related to Inō Tadataka. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2006-03-01. Kept at the Inō Tadataka Memorial Museum (Sawara-i).

name_romaji: Kyū Sekidomachi no Sarutahiko Tōbu oyobi Ryōtebu
name_en: Head and Hands of the Sarutahiko Figure of Former Sekido Town
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property, the head and both hands of the Sarutahiko festival figure of former Sekido town. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2006-03-01. Kept at the Suigō Sawara Dashi Kaikan (Sawara-i).

name_en: Artifacts Excavated from Ōto Miyasaku Tumulus No. 1
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, artifacts excavated from Ōto Miyasaku Tumulus No. 1. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2007-07-03. Kept at the Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Hall (Hanegawa).

name_en: Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Tumulus
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama burial mound. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2014-06-02. Held privately and by the Sannowakeme district (Sannowakeme).

name_en: Hōkyōin Stone Pagoda of Raikōji Temple
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the hōkyōintō stone pagoda of Raikōji Temple. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2015-07-30. Held privately and by the Kaizuka district (Kaizuka).

name_en: Materials Held by the Ōnegi Family
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, materials held by the Ōnegi family. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2016-09-05. Privately held (Katori).

name_en: Great Kagura Dance of Makino
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property, the daikagura (great sacred dance) of Makino. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2019-02-01. Maintained by the Makino Kagura Preservation Society (Makino).

name_en: Statues of the Eight Dragon Deities of Katori Jingū Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, statues of the eight dragon deities of Katori Jingū Shrine. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2021-06-01. Held by Katori Jingū Shrine (Katori).

name_en: Paired Stone Slab Monument at the Site of Tahōin Manpukuji Temple
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a paired (twin-form) stone slab monument (itabi) at the site of Tahōin Manpukuji Temple. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2022-03-01. Held by the Kiuchi district (Kiuchi).

name_en: Stone Slab Monument Inscribed with the Second Year of Shōka
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a stone slab monument (itabi) inscribed with the second year of the Shōka era. It was municipally designated by Katori City on 2022-03-01. Privately held (Yanaka).

name_en: Five-Ring Stone Pagoda
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, a five-ring stone pagoda (gorintō). It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1966. Located in Todorokichō, Inage Ward.

name_en: Five-Ring Stone Pagoda of Itsukadō
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the five-ring stone pagoda (gorintō) of Itsukadō. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Hondachō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Five-Ring Stone Pagoda Attributed to Chiba Munetane
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, a five-ring stone pagoda (gorintō) traditionally attributed to Chiba Munetane. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Benten, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Stone Five-Ring Pagoda of Jajashiki
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the stone five-ring pagoda (gorintō) of Jajashiki. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1988. Located in Ochichō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Former Oihama Town Hall
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the former town hall of Oihama. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1994. Located in Hamanochō, Chūō Ward.

name_romaji: Kyū Kawasaki Ginkō Chiba Shiten Honkan
name_en: Former Kawasaki Bank Chiba Branch Main Building
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, the main building of the former Kawasaki Bank Chiba branch. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1995. Located in Chūō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Seated Statue of Dainichi Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a seated statue of Dainichi Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1961. Located in Makuharichō, Hanamigawa Ward.

name_en: Standing Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a standing statue of Amida Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1961. Located in Makuharichō, Hanamigawa Ward.

name_en: Statue of Yakushi Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a statue of Yakushi Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1962. Located in Takashinachō, Wakaba Ward.

name_en: Statue of Gakkō Bosatsu
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a statue of Gakkō Bosatsu. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1962. Located in Takashinachō, Wakaba Ward.

name_en: Carved Panel Inset in a Frieze Wall
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a carved panel inset in a frieze wall. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Nobuto, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Zenkōji-Style Standing Statue of Kannon
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a Zenkōji-style standing statue of Kannon. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Hirayamachō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Komagata Great Buddha of Naganuma
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, the Komagata Great Buddha of Naganuma. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1975. Located in Naganumachō, Inage Ward.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Myōken Bosatsu
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden standing statue of Myōken Bosatsu. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1988. Located in Ōmiyachō, Wakaba Ward.

name_en: Two Wooden Standing Statues of Deva Figures
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, two wooden standing statues of deva (tenbu) figures. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1999. Located in Hirayamachō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1999. Located in Kawadochō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Seishi Bosatsu
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden standing statue of Seishi Bosatsu. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1999. Located in Oyumino-Ariyoshi, Midori Ward.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Shaka Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden seated statue of Shaka Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1999. Located in Nitonachō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2000. Located in Todorokichō, Inage Ward.

name_en: Wooden Head of Fudō Myōō
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, the wooden head of a statue of Fudō Myōō. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2000. Located in Chūō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Bronze Seated Statue of the Thousand-Armed Kannon Bosatsu
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a bronze seated statue of the thousand-armed Kannon Bosatsu. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2000. Located in Ichibachō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2000. Located in Oyumichō, Chūō Ward.
